class.name: SuperMarker3D
class.brief_description: Graphic-design-oriented 3D marker node — five marker types (Axis, Mesh, Shape, Curve, Figure) covering 26 subtype variants, with universal outline / fill / detail-mode controls.
class.description:
SuperMarker3D is a standalone 3D marker / annotation node. Drop one into any scene as a Node3D child; everything is configured from the inspector or script. No companion plugin required — though it cooperates with MultiNode as a template child (set [member SuperMarker3D.template_mode] via [method set_template_mode] and the instancer will draw per-instance copies).
[b]Type / subtype model[/b] The top-level [member type] dropdown picks a [enum MarkerType] category. The [member subtype] dropdown then narrows to the variants in that category — [code]CROSS / PLAIN / BURR / XYZ[/code] for Axis, [code]SPHERE / BOX / DIAMOND / CYLINDER / CONE / CAPSULE[/code] for Mesh, [code]CIRCLE / SQUARE / DIAMOND / TRIANGLE / CAPSULE / X / ARROW_FLAT[/code] for Shape, [code]LINE / RIGHT_ANGLE / ARC / SINE / HELIX / BEZIER / CUSTOM[/code] for Curve, and [code]FIGURE[/code] for Figure. The inspector hides properties that don't apply to the current type.
[b]Detail modes[/b] [b]DETAIL_WIREFRAME[/b] — filled solid mesh (back-culled) with thin face-normal edge quads, so only camera-front-facing edges are visible — looks like a real mesh at any angle. [b]DETAIL_SILHOUETTE[/b] — billboarded 2D outline + optional fill, always faces the camera.
[b]API stability[/b] Pre-1.0: enum reordering, name changes, and removals are permitted between minor versions. From 1.0 forward: enum integer values are frozen for scene-file compatibility, no renames, no removals without a deprecation cycle.
[b]Minimal script example[/b] [codeblock] var m := SuperMarker3D.new() m.type = SuperMarker3D.TYPE_MESH m.subtype = SuperMarker3D.MESH_SPHERE m.marker_size = 0.75 m.outline_color = Color.YELLOW m.fill_color = Color(0.0, 0.6, 1.0, 0.5) add_child(m) [/codeblock]

method.name: export_mesh
method.description: Returns a freestanding [ArrayMesh] duplicate of every surface currently rendered by this marker (the primary mesh and, for Axis subtypes, each per-arm surface). Drop the result into a [MeshInstance3D], save it to disk, etc. Geometry is in marker-local space — apply the marker's transform when consuming the result if you want world-space. No materials are attached.

method.name: export_convex_shape
method.description: Convex-hull collision shape covering every triangle vertex of the marker's geometry. Cheap and stable at runtime; concavities are filled in. Suitable for movable physics bodies.

method.name: export_concave_shape
method.description: Triangle-mesh collision shape that matches the marker's visuals exactly — keeps helix coils, right-angle bends, arc curves, etc. Static-only (no movement under physics) and pricier per query than the convex variant.

method.name: get_active_curve
method.description: Returns a [b]duplicate[/b] of the [Curve3D] currently driving the geometry — the user-supplied [member curve] resource for [code]CURVE_CUSTOM[/code], or a freshly generated curve built from the active preset's parameters for any other Curve subtype. The duplicate is independent: edit it, save it as a [code].tres[/code], or hand it to a [Path3D] for an AI agent / camera to follow without affecting the marker. Returns an empty Curve3D if the marker isn't a Curve subtype.

method.name: get_mesh_rid
method.description: Returns the RenderingServer mesh RID currently held by this marker. Useful when wiring a SuperMarker3D into another instancer (e.g. MultiNode's per-instance mesh ID slot).

method.name: is_template_mode
method.description: Returns true when this marker has been flipped into template mode by an external instancer — see [method set_template_mode].

method.name: reset_interpolation
method.description: Reset the physics interpolation state so the next render frame draws at the current transform with no blend from the previous one. Call after teleporting this marker (or its parent) to a new pose — otherwise the engine will draw a one-frame smear from the old position to the new one.

method.name: set_template_mode
method.description: Flip the marker into "template" mode — it stops rendering itself, but its mesh / materials remain available for an external instancer to draw. MultiNode's SuperMarkerHandler calls this at scan time so a SuperMarker3D used as a template child doesn't double-render alongside its stamped instances. Not normally called from game code.

members.member: Top-level marker category. Picking a type narrows the [member subtype] dropdown to variants in that category, and toggles the inspector visibility of every type-specific group below. Changing type re-initialises type-specific data fields to their defaults.
Variant within the current [member type]. Enum integer values are frozen for scene-file compatibility — old pre-1.0 scenes store these directly and keep their meaning.
Universal edge / outline color. Used by every subtype that draws an outline strip.

Universal outline width in meters. At [code]0.0[/code], outlines render as 1px lines (crisp at every camera distance). Above 0, axis arms become thin tubes, arrowheads gain volume, and mesh-face outlines paint as a strip of the given thickness. Range hint allows values greater than 1m for dramatic stand-ins (overthick green burr as a bush, etc.).
Overall size in meters for Mesh and Shape subtypes. Hidden for Axis (uses per-direction lengths) and Figure (uses [member figure_height]).
Body length between the two hemisphere caps for [code]MESH_CAPSULE[/code], and the body length of the 2D pill for [code]FLAT_CAPSULE[/code]. Cap radius follows [member marker_size].
[code]DETAIL_WIREFRAME[/code] (0) for a real-mesh look with face-normal edges; [code]DETAIL_SILHOUETTE[/code] (1) for a billboarded 2D outline that always faces the camera.
Solid-body color. Alpha < 1 makes the marker translucent; alpha = 0 hides the fill entirely (outline-only).
Curve subtypes only: color of the gap panels between dashes / dots. Transparent by default so gaps are invisible.

Linkage between the six [code]axis_length_*[/code] fields. [code]LINK_ALL[/code] drives all six from a single value (uniform marker). [code]LINK_MIRRORED[/code] couples each axis's pos and neg directions. [code]LINK_FREE[/code] leaves every direction independent. Shared across every Axis subtype so switching between Cross / Plain / Burr / XYZ preserves authored values and link behavior.
Length of the +X axis arm. Set to 0 to hide this arm. See [member axis_link_mode] for which fields are slaved.
Length of the -X axis arm. Set to 0 to hide this arm.
Length of the +Y axis arm. Set to 0 to hide this arm.
Length of the -Y axis arm. Set to 0 to hide this arm.
Length of the +Z axis arm. Set to 0 to hide this arm.
Length of the -Z axis arm. Set to 0 to hide this arm. Note: [code]AXIS_CROSS[/code] disables Z entirely.

Add a cone arrowhead to the tip of every non-zero axis arm. Honored by [code]AXIS_CROSS[/code], [code]AXIS_PLAIN[/code], and [code]AXIS_XYZ[/code]; ignored by [code]AXIS_BURR[/code] (the diagonals make it visually noisy).
Arrowhead cone height in meters, measured back from the arm tip along the arm direction.
Arrowhead base radius (splay perpendicular to the arm).
[code]AXIS_XYZ[/code] only: bright red on the +X arm by default. Per-arm color survives the line→tube switch at thickness > 0.
[code]AXIS_XYZ[/code] only: dark red on the -X arm by default.
[code]AXIS_XYZ[/code] only: bright green on the +Y arm by default.
[code]AXIS_XYZ[/code] only: dark green on the -Y arm by default.
[code]AXIS_XYZ[/code] only: bright blue on the +Z arm by default.
[code]AXIS_XYZ[/code] only: dark blue on the -Z arm by default.
Standing tip-to-toe extent of the Figure subtype, in meters. Body proportions are derived from this value.
When true, the skinned humanoid mesh renders. Turn off to see only the rig bones (combine with [member figure_show_bones]).

Rigging mode toggle. When true: pose rotations are forced to zero, per-bone rest rotation / length / width and the offsets become inspector-visible, and a tube overlay of every bone renders on top of the rest mesh. When false: pose rotations drive the chain, the mesh renders posed, and rig fields are hidden.

Color of the bone-overlay tubes drawn when [member figure_show_bones] is on. Independent from fill / outline color so the mesh and the rig overlay can be coloured separately. Alpha < 1 lets the user see bones through the mesh while rigging.
Rigging-only offset from the spine tip to the head pivot, in the spine's rotated frame.
Capsule radius of the head-base offset bar — drives both the rig visualizer thickness and the skinning Voronoi at this joint.
Rigging-only offset from the spine tip to the left shoulder pivot.
Capsule radius of the left-shoulder offset bar.
Rigging-only offset from the spine tip to the right shoulder pivot.
Capsule radius of the right-shoulder offset bar.
Rigging-only offset from the pelvis to the left hip pivot.
Capsule radius of the left-hip offset bar.
Rigging-only offset from the pelvis to the right hip pivot.
Capsule radius of the right-hip offset bar.
Pelvis position in the marker's local space (rigging-only, baked).
Pelvis bone radius — affects rig visualizer and the skinning Voronoi.
Rest rotation of the spine bone (Euler XYZ). Rigging-only.
Rest length of the spine bone in meters.
Spine bone radius.
Pose rotation of the spine bone (Euler XYZ), applied on top of the rest rotation. Zero = rest pose. This is the runtime animation control.
Rest rotation of the head bone.
Rest length of the head bone.
Head bone radius.
Pose rotation of the head bone.
Rest rotation of the left upper arm.
Rest length of the left upper arm.
Left upper arm radius.
Pose rotation of the left upper arm.
Rest rotation of the left lower arm.
Rest length of the left lower arm.
Left lower arm radius.
Pose rotation of the left lower arm.
Rest rotation of the right upper arm.
Rest length of the right upper arm.
Right upper arm radius.
Pose rotation of the right upper arm.
Rest rotation of the right lower arm.
Rest length of the right lower arm.
Right lower arm radius.
Pose rotation of the right lower arm.
Rest rotation of the left upper leg.
Rest length of the left upper leg.
Left upper leg radius.
Pose rotation of the left upper leg.
Rest rotation of the left lower leg.
Rest length of the left lower leg.
Left lower leg radius.
Pose rotation of the left lower leg.
Rest rotation of the right upper leg.
Rest length of the right upper leg.
Right upper leg radius.
Pose rotation of the right upper leg.
Rest rotation of the right lower leg.
Rest length of the right lower leg.
Right lower leg radius.
Pose rotation of the right lower leg.
[code]ARROW_FLAT[/code] only: arrowhead length in marker-local units, measured back from the arrow tip.
[code]ARROW_FLAT[/code] only: full arrowhead width at the shoulders.
Curve only: when true the curve renders as a flat ribbon (2D-look stamp on a 3D path); when false it renders as a 3D tube.
Curve preset only: overall length of the generated path (semantics vary slightly by preset — straight-line distance for [code]CURVE_LINE[/code], arc chord, helix axial length, etc.).
Curve preset only: bend / radius / wave depth for presets that have one (arc radius, sine height, helix radius, bezier deflection).
Curve preset only: cycle count for periodic presets (sine cycles, helix turns).
Curve preset only: tessellation segment count along the path. Higher = smoother but more verts.
User-supplied [Curve3D] driving the path when [member subtype] is [code]CURVE_CUSTOM[/code]. Ignored for the procedural presets — see [method get_active_curve] to get the actual curve in use.
Curve only: ribbon / tube width (flat) or tube radius (3D) in meters.
Curve only: lean angle of the ribbon around its travel direction, in radians. Useful for banking a racetrack ribbon into turns.
Curve only: ease-in / ease-out fraction at the ends of the bank ramp (0 = no easing, hard step; 0.5 = full sigmoid).
Curve only: [code]CURVE_PATTERN_SOLID[/code], [code]CURVE_PATTERN_DASH[/code], or [code]CURVE_PATTERN_DOT[/code].
Curve only: length of one dash segment when [member curve_pattern] is [code]DASH[/code].
Curve only: length of the gap between dashes / dots.
Curve only: cap style at the start of the path — [code]None / Arrow / Dot / Line[/code].
Curve only: cap dimensions at the start. x = along-path length, y = perpendicular width. When [member start_cap_linked] is true, the setter mirrors x into y so the cap stays proportional.
Curve only: when true, [member start_cap_size] keeps x and y synchronized — editing one updates the other.
Curve only: cap style at the end of the path.
Curve only: cap dimensions at the end. See [member start_cap_size].
Curve only: when true, [member end_cap_size] keeps x and y synchronized.
Curve only: fraction (0–1) of the path that is actually rendered. Useful for "drawing in" a path over time — animate from 0 to 1 with a Tween.

Mesh only: longitudinal segment count for sphere / diamond / cone / cylinder / capsule. Range 5..24. Controls both fill tessellation and wireframe segmentation. Use [code]mesh_sides = 4[/code] on [code]MESH_CONE[/code] for a four-sided pyramid.

Mesh only: when true (default), curved subtypes (sphere, diamond, cone, cylinder, capsule) use smooth per-pixel normals plus the analytical sphere shader for lat/lon (or meridian + rim) outlines. When false, every face uses its flat face normal and outlines are painted along the actual mesh edges — the faceted, low-poly look. Pyramid is always faceted regardless.

Render both sides of the geometry. Back faces draw in an earlier pass (cull_front, no depth-write, lower render_priority) and front faces overwrite them where they overlap — so the mesh reads correctly from all angles at any fill alpha. Flat-shape subtypes use a single cull-disabled surface instead.
When [member two_sided] is false, renders the back face instead of the front face.

Checkerboard overlay. When on, multiplies a darken factor into alternate squares of a triplanar grid in marker-local space — useful for showing real-world scale on Mesh / Shape / Curve / Figure / Axis tubes while prototyping a level.
Checker only: edge length of one square, in the same units as the marker's local space.
Checker only: multiplier applied to alternating squares (0 = black, 1 = no change).
Shape only: when true, the flat shape rotates in the XZ plane to face the camera while keeping Y axis fixed (BILLBOARD_FIXED_Y). Mutually distinct from [member billboard_y].
Shape only: when true, the flat shape fully faces the camera (BILLBOARD_ENABLED).
Shape only: when true, arc joints and polygon corners gain a disc blob to produce smooth rounded joins.
[code]FLAT_CIRCLE[/code] only: regular N-gon segment count. Range 6..64. Hidden for all other subtypes.
Renderer flag — when false, the marker hides at runtime (only visible in the editor). Pair with [member always_on_top] for editor-only annotations.
Renderer flag — when true, disables depth testing so the marker draws over opaque geometry. Useful for editor cues and HUD overlays.

Renderer flag — when true, the marker switches to per-pixel lit shading and casts / receives shadows like a normal MeshInstance3D. A thick burr stands in for a bush, a tall mesh box stands in for a building, etc. Off (default) = unshaded, no shadows — best for editor cues, HUD overlays, and pure design markers.

constants.constant: Axis category — line clusters through the origin (Cross / Plain / Burr / XYZ).
Mesh category — closed 3D primitives (sphere, box, diamond, cylinder, cone, capsule).
Shape category — flat 2D polygon icons + the flat arrow.
Curve category — path stamped along a [Curve3D] (procedural presets + user-supplied custom).
Figure category — humanoid mock-up with an 11-bone rig.
Four arms on the X/Y plane (Z disabled).
Six axes (±X ±Y ±Z), all rendered in [member outline_color].
Flat 2D arrow icon — lives in the Shape category.
Six axes with per-direction colors (bright on positive, dark on negative by default).
Six cardinal + six face-diagonal axes (12 lines total).
Round-base cone. Use [member mesh_sides] = 4 for a four-sided pyramid.
Cylinder body + hemisphere caps. See [member capsule_height].
Regular N-gon, segment count from [member shape_sides].
2D pill — two semicircles + rectangular body. See [member capsule_height].
A single value drives all six axis lengths.
Pos and neg of the same axis share a value; each axis pair is independent.
Filled mesh + face-normal edge quads (camera-front edges only). Looks like a real mesh.
Billboarded 2D outline + optional fill. Always faces the camera.
Root bone — position is [member figure_bone_pelvis_pos], rotation is the node transform.
